items.title: Gradient-based optimization
items.description: Training ML models minimizes loss by following negative derivatives (gradients) of the loss with respect to parameters.

items.title: Activation function analysis
items.description: Derivatives of ReLU, sigmoid and tanh determine how fast signals and errors propagate through layers during backpropagation.

items.title: Learning rate scheduling
items.description: Magnitude of derivatives (or gradients) informs step sizes—large slopes may need smaller learning rates for stability.

items.title: Sensitivity and interpretability
items.description: Derivatives of model outputs with respect to inputs quantify local sensitivity (related to saliency and influence functions).
